mnemonics: Message Call: Like sending a text to a smart contract, asking it to do something (e.g., transfer tokens), but the 'reply' (state change) is written on the blockchain ledger.
keyPoints: A message call is an internal transaction initiated by a smart contract, not signed by an external user.
It is used to invoke a function within another smart contract or to transfer Ether to an account.
Message calls do not create a new contract and are not recorded as standalone transactions on the blockchain; they are execution steps inside a parent transaction.
commonMistakes: Confusing a message call (internal) with a standard transaction (external, signed and broadcast).
Assuming a message call will create a new contract, which is the function of a contract creation transaction.
